verses.sanskrit: रामः प्रेक्ष्य तु तम् गृध्रम् भुवि रौद्रेण पातितम् ।सौमित्रिम् मित्र संपन्नम् इदम् वचनम् अब्रवीत् ॥३-६८-१॥
verses.text:
Rama on seeing that eagle felled to ground by the atrocious Ravana, said this sentence to Soumitri who 
is an asset for good companionability. [3-68-1]

verses.sanskrit: मम अयम् नूनम् अर्थेषु यतमानो विहंगमः ।राक्षसेन हतः संख्ये प्राणान् त्यजति मत् कृते ॥३-६८-२॥
verses.text:
"While this bird is venturing in my concern the demon felled him in combat and he is leaving off his 
lives owing to me, definitely... [3-68-2]

verses.sanskrit: अति खिन्नः शरीरे अस्मिन् प्राणो लक्ष्मण विद्यते ।तथा स्वर विहीनो अयम् विक्लवम् समुदीक्षते ॥३-६८-३॥
verses.text:
"Obviously, lives in his body are very feeble, oh, Lakshmana, it is natural, isn't it, and he looks 
highly anguished with a dulled voice..." Thus on saying to Lakshmana, Rama then spoke to Jataayu. [3-68-3]

verses.sanskrit: जटायो यदि शक्नोषि वाक्यम् व्याहरितुम् पुनः ।सीताम् आख्याहि भद्रम् ते वधम् आख्याहि च आत्मनः ॥३-६८-४॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Jataayu, if you are in fine fettle to further reiterate words, relate about Seetha, let safe betide 
you, and recount how you are murdered, too... [3-68-4]

verses.sanskrit: किम् निमित्तो जहार आर्याम् रावणः तस्य किम् मया ।अपराधम् तु यम् दृष्ट्वा रावणेन हृता प्रिया ॥३-६८-५॥
verses.text:
"What is the reason for Ravana in stealing that noblewoman, and even what is the wrong I have committed 
in his respect, keeping which in view that Ravana stole my ladylove... [3-68-5]

verses.sanskrit: कथम् तत् चन्द्र संकाशम् मुखम् आसीत् मनोहरम् ।सीतया कानि च उक्तानि तस्मिन् काले द्विजोत्तम ॥३-६८-६॥
verses.text:
"How was that moon similar heart-stealing face of hers at that time of abduction, oh, best bird, and 
even what are the words she uttered when being abducted... [3-68-6]

verses.sanskrit: कथम् वीर्यः कथम् रूपः किम् कर्मा स च राक्षसः ।क्व च अस्य भवनम् तात ब्रूहि मे परिपृच्छतः ॥३-६८-७॥
verses.text:
"What is his stamina of that demon? What is his form? And what are his strategies? And where is his 
stronghold? Oh, sire, tell me while I ask you inquisitively..." Rama urged Jataayu thus for information. 
[3-68-7]

verses.sanskrit: तम् उद्वीक्ष्य सः धर्मात्मा विलपन्तम् अनाथवत् ।वाचा विक्लवया रामम् इदम् वचनम् अब्रवीत् ॥३-६८-८॥
verses.text:
Then that warm-hearted Jataayu with his eyes rolling upwards saw Rama and with a fluttery voice said 
this sentence to Rama who is wailing like an orphan. [3-68-8]

verses.sanskrit: सा हृता राक्षसेन्द्रेण रावणेन दुरात्मना ।मायाम् आस्थाय विपुलाम् वात दुर्दिन संकुलाम् ॥३-६८-९॥
verses.text:
"Resorting to his illusionistic devices like creating an immense and tumultuous windstorm and cloud-cover, 
the flagitious chief of demons Ravana abducted Seetha... [3-68-9]

verses.sanskrit: परिक्लांतस्य मे तात पक्षौ चित्त्वा निशाचरः ।सीताम् आदाय वैदेहीम् प्रयातो दक्षिणा मुखः ॥३-६८-१०॥
verses.text:
"That night walker hacked both my wings when I was overtired, and went southward taking the princess 
of Videha, Seetha, along with him... [3-68-10]

verses.sanskrit: उपरुध्यन्ति मे प्राणा दृष्टिर् भ्रमति राघव ।पश्यामि वृक्षान् सौवर्णान् उशीर कृत मूर्धजान् ॥३-६८-११॥
verses.text:
"My sight is spinning and my lives are stifled, oh, Raghava, I am now seeing golden trees with cuscus 
grass as their treetops... [3-68-11]

verses.sanskrit: येन याति मुहूर्तेन सीताम् आदाय रावणः ।विप्रनष्टम् धनम् क्षिप्रम् तत् स्वामि प्रतिपद्यते ॥३-६८-१२॥
verses.text:
"In which spell of time Ravana has gone taking away Seetha, that spell is named as Vinda... if any riches 
are lost during that spell, the original possessor of those riches will repossess them very quickly... 
oh, Kakutstha, he that Ravana is unmindful of that fact and stole Seetha only to loose her... [3-68-12, 
13a]

verses.sanskrit: विन्दो नाम मुहूर्तो असौ स च काकुत्स्थ न अबुधत् ।त्वत् प्रियाम् जानकीम् हृत्वा रावणो राक्षसेश्वर ।झषवत् बडिशम् गृह्य क्षिप्रम् एव विनश्यति ॥३-६८-१३॥
verses.text:
"On stealing your ladylove Janaki, that chief of demons, Ravana, will ruin himself, as with a fish which 
swallows a baited fishhook... [3-68-13b, c]

verses.sanskrit: न च त्वया व्यथा कार्या जनकस्य सुताम् प्रति ।वैदेह्या रंस्यसे क्षिप्रम् हत्वा तम् रणमूर्धनि ॥३-६८-१४॥
verses.text:
"Impractical is your anguishing for the daughter of Janaka, as you will delight yourself with Vaidehi 
in no time, on killing that Ravana in the vanguard of a war..." Thus saying Jataayu paused for a while. 
[3-68-14]

verses.sanskrit: असंमूढस्य गृध्रस्य रामम् प्रति अनुभाषतः ।आस्यात् सुस्राव रुधिरम् म्रियमाणस्य स अमिषम् ॥३-६८-१५॥
verses.text:
Though Jataayu is speaking on to Rama with an alert mind, blood freely flowed from his mouth with shreds 
of flesh as death verged on him, even then he struggled to say. [3-68-15]

verses.sanskrit: पुत्रो विश्रवसः साक्षात् भ्राता वैश्रवणस्य च ।इति उक्त्वा दुर्लभान् प्राणान् मुमोच पतगेश्वरः ॥३-६८-१६॥
verses.text:
"That demon is the son of Vishravasa... literally... the brother of Kubera..." on saying thus, that 
lord of birds released his lives, which are unobtainable for oneself. [3-68-16]

verses.sanskrit: ब्रूहि ब्रूहि इति रामस्य ब्रुवाणस्य कृतांजलेः ।त्यक्त्वा शरीरम् गृध्रस्य जग्मुः प्राणा विहायसम् ॥३-६८-१७॥
verses.text:
But the lives of Jataayu departing from his body went into thin air before the very eyes of Rama who 
is still speaking to him with folded-palms, "tell... tell more..." [3-68-17]

verses.sanskrit: स निक्षिप्य शिरो भूमौ प्रसार्य चरणौ तदा ।विक्षिप्य च शरीरम् स्वम् पपात धरणी तले ॥३-६८-१८॥
verses.text:
Jataayu flopped his head on earth, sprawled his feet on ground, and then his body collapsed onto the 
surface of earth writhing jerkily. [3-68-18]

verses.sanskrit: तम् गृध्रम् प्रेक्ष्य ताम्र अक्षम् गत असुम् अचलोपमम् ।रामः सु बहुभिः दुह्खैः दीनः सौमित्रिम् अब्रवीत् ॥३-६८-१९॥
verses.text:
He who is worsened by very many mishaps that Rama, on seeing the mountainous eagle whose eyes are bloodshot 
and whose lives have gone, said this way to Soumitri... [3-68-19]

verses.sanskrit: बहूनि रक्षसाम् वासे वर्षाणि वसता सुखम् ।अनेन दण्डकारण्ये विशीर्णम् इह पक्षिणा ॥३-६८-२०॥
verses.text:
"This bird which for years together lived fearlessly in Dandaka forest, a habitat of demons, that bird 
wilted to death, because of me... [3-68-20]

verses.sanskrit: अनेक वार्षिको यः तु चिर काल समुत्थितः ।सो अयम् अद्य हतः शेते कालो हि दुर्अतिक्रमः ॥३-६८-२१॥
verses.text:
"He who has longevity over many years, who actively strived for a long for rectitude, he is now killed 
and prostrating before the Time, because of me... alas... it is indeed impossible to infringe the Time... 
[3-68-21]

verses.sanskrit: पश्य लक्ष्मण गृध्रो अयम् उपकारी हतः च मे ।सीताम् अभ्यवपन्नो हि रावणेन बलीयसा ॥३-६८-२२॥
verses.text:
"Lakshmana, this eagle which has dashed to rescue Seetha in order to render help to me is slain by that 
brute-forced Ravana, only because of me, you see... [3-68-22]

verses.sanskrit: गृध्र राज्यम् परित्यज्य पितृ पैतामहम् महत् ।मम हेतोः अयम् प्राणान् मुमोच पतगेश्वरः ॥३-६८-२३॥
verses.text:
"On abandoning great empire of eagles belonging to his father and forefathers this lord of bird has 
now abandoned his lives too, only for my sake... [3-68-23]

verses.sanskrit: सर्वत्र खलु दृश्यन्ते साधवो धर्म चारिणः ।शूराः शरण्याः सौमित्रे तिर्यक् योनि गतेषु अपि ॥३-६८-२४॥
verses.text:
"Universally noticeable are the protective, prepotent, principled ones that are the followers of probity, 
oh, Soumitri, even though their births are avian or bestial... [3-68-24]

verses.sanskrit: सीता हरणजं दुःखम् न मे सौम्य तथा गतम् ।यथा विनाशो गृध्रस्य मत् कृते च परंतप ॥३-६८-२५॥
verses.text:
"To me, oh, enemy-inflamer, oh, gentle Lakshmana, anguish caused by Seetha's abduction is not that much, 
when compared with the anguish caused by the perish of this eagle, that too, because of me... [3-68-25]

verses.sanskrit: राजा दशरथः श्रीमान् यथा मम मया यशाः ।पूजनीयः च मान्यः च तथा अयम् पतगेश्वरः ॥३-६८-२६॥
verses.text:
"As to how the celebrated and highly renowned king Dasharatha is venerable and honourable for me, likewise 
this lord of birds is also a venerable and honourable one to me... [3-68-26]

verses.sanskrit: सौमित्रे हर काष्ठानि निर्मथिष्यामि पावकम् ।गृध्र राजम् दिधक्षामि मत् कृते निधनम् गतम् ॥३-६८-२७॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Soumitri, get the firewood and I will produce fire by friction of two sticks, as I wish to cremate 
this lord of birds who expired because of me... [3-68-27]

verses.sanskrit: नाथम् पतग लोकस्य चिताम् आरोपयामि अहम् ।इमम् धक्ष्यामि सौमित्रे हतम् रौद्रेण रक्षसा ॥३-६८-२८॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Soumitri, I will mount this lord of realm of birds onto the pyre and cremate him who is killed 
by a feral demon..." Thus saying to Lakshmana, Rama said this to dead eagle Jataayu. [3-68-28]

verses.sanskrit: या गतिः यज्ञ शीलानाम् आहित अग्नेः च या गतिः ।अ पर आवर्तिनाम् या च या च भूमि प्रदायिनाम् ॥३-६८-२९॥
verses.text:
"Oh, greatly mighty king of eagles, by me cremated ritually and by me aptly consented to, you depart 
to the unexcelled heavenly worlds... you depart to those worlds that are destined for the virtuosos 
of Vedic-rituals, and to those worlds that are destined for the practisers of ascesis amid Five-Ritual-fires, 
and to those that are destined for un-retreating combatants, and to those worlds that destined for the 
donors of lands..." So said Rama to the departed Jataayu. [3-68-29, 30]

verses.sanskrit: मया त्वम् समनुज्ञातो गच्छ लोकान् अनुत्तमान् ।गृध्र राज महा सत्त्व संस्कृतः च मया व्रज ॥३-६८-३०॥
एवम् उक्त्वा चिताम् दीप्ताम् आरोप्य पतगेश्वरम् ।ददाह रामो धर्मात्मा स्व बन्धुम् इव दुःखितः ॥३-६८-३१॥
verses.text:
On saying that way, that ethical-souled Rama mounted that lord of birds onto the pyre and he sorrowfully 
incinerated that eagle in a flaring fire of pyre, as he would do in respect of his own deceased relative. 
[3-68-31]

verses.sanskrit: रामो अथ सह सौमित्रिः वनम् यात्वा स वीर्यवान् ।स्थूलान् हत्वा महा रोहीन् अनु तस्तार तम् द्विजम् ॥३-६८-३२॥
verses.text:
Then that resolute Rama on going into forest along with Soumitri hunted a robust-bodied, big Rohi animal, 
or, Kesari animal, and then he spread sacred grass on ground to place that offering to the deceased 
soul of that bird. [3-68-32]

verses.sanskrit: रोहि मांसानि च उद्धृत्य पेशी कृत्वा महायशाः ।शकुनाय ददौ रामो रम्ये हरित शाद्वले ॥३-६८-३३॥
verses.text:
On drawing up the flesh of that Rohi animal and lumping it to gobbets, that highly observant Rama placed 
those gobbets on pleasant greenish pasturelands as obsequial offerings in respect of that bird Jataayu. 
[3-68-33]

verses.sanskrit: यत् तत् प्रेतस्य मर्त्यस्य कथयन्ति द्विजातयः ।तत् स्वर्ग गमनम् पित्र्यम् क्षिप्रम् रामो जजाप ह ॥३-६८-३४॥
verses.text:
Rama immediately chanted Vedic hymns that are employed in such funerals of one's own paternal people, 
as Brahmans say that those hymns are employable in such rites as they lead the soul of the departed 
to heaven. [3-68-34]

verses.sanskrit: ततो गोदावरीम् गत्वा नदीम् नर वर आत्मजौ ।उदकम् चक्रतुः तस्मै गृध्र राजाय तौ उभौ ॥३-६८-३५॥
verses.text:
Then both the sons of that notable man, namely Dasharatha, on going to River Godavari they have oblated 
waters for that king of eagles, Jataayu. [3-68-35]

verses.sanskrit: शास्त्र दृष्टेन विधिना जले गृधाय राघवौ ।स्नात्वा तौ गृध्र राजाय उदकम् चक्रुः तदा ॥३-६८-३६॥
verses.text:
Both of the Raghava-s took funeral baths in the waters of River Godavari and then made water oblations 
to the king of eagles. [3-68-36]

verses.sanskrit: स गृध्र राजः कृतवान् यशस्करम्सु दुष्करम् कर्म रणे निपातितः ।महर्षि कल्पेन च संस्कृतः तदाजगाम पुण्याम् गतिम् आत्मनः शुभाम् ॥३-६८-३७॥
verses.text:
That king of eagle Jataayu, who has performed a creditworthy deed of stalling and combating Ravana, 
but who is felled by that Ravana, went away to the merited and auspicious heavenly realms of his own, 
as and when consecrated by sublime sage like Rama. [3-68-37]

verses.sanskrit: कृतोदकौ तौ अपि पक्षि सत्तमेस्थिराम् च बुद्धिम् प्रणिधाय जग्मुतुः ।प्रवेश्य सीता अधिगमने ततो मनोवनम् सुरेन्द्रौ इव विष्णु वासवौ ॥३-६८-३८॥
verses.text:
Even those two, Rama and Lakshmana, on performing water oblations in respect of that best bird Jataayu, 
and on assertively giving thought to the information given by Jataayu, they like the chiefs of gods, 
namely Vishnu and Indra, went to southerly woods when their mind is set to search for Seetha. [3-68-38]
